# MOTION.md
# Generated by: npx @edmill/motion-md init --preset linear
# Drop this file in your project root.
# AI agents (Claude Code, Cursor) read this before generating animated components.
# Edit values directly or regenerate at: https://craftedby.ai/motion-md

---
motion:
  enter:
    duration: 180ms # short3
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.05,0.7,0.1,1)
    token: emphasized-decelerate
    direction: rise
    distance: 8px
    exit-duration: 100ms
---

## Element enter

Fast arrival, minimal travel. Elements pop in with purpose — no lingering, no ceremony.

---
motion:
  notification:
    duration: 500ms # long2
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)
    token: custom-spring
    slide-from: bottom
    distance: 48px
    exit-duration: 280ms
---

## Notification

Notifications should arrive with energy — they're interrupting the user for a reason.
The spring overshoot makes the card feel alive, like it demands to be noticed.
It springs up from below and lands with a satisfying bounce that says "hey, look at me."

---
motion:
  press:
    duration: 80ms # short1
    easing: cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1)
    token: custom-spring
    scale-down: 0.96
---

## Button press

Spring rebound makes the press feel mechanical — like a physical key clicking back.

---
component-overrides:
  # Framer Motion — paste these transition objects directly into components
  framer.notification:
    initial: '{ opacity: 0, y: 48 }'
    animate: '{ opacity: 1, y: 0, transition: { duration: 0.5, ease: [0.34,1.56,0.64,1] } }'
    exit:    '{ opacity: 0, y: 24, transition: { duration: 0.28 } }'

  framer.press:
    whileTap: '{ scale: 0.96, transition: { duration: 0.08, ease: [0.34,1.56,0.64,1] } }'
---

## Generation notes

- Notification recipe uses spring easing — overshoot is intentional and on-brand
- slide-from: bottom means notifications arrive from the bottom of the viewport
- distance: 48px is the starting Y offset before the spring animation
- Exit is 56% of enter duration — things leave faster than they arrive
